description: Extracts a single item from a collection by index. Works with polygons (vertex, edge, angle), text collections (select, selectexcept, selectcell), tables (row, col), and lines (angle, normal, foot).
utility: Extract an individual component from a polygon, text collection, table, or line

arguments.name: typeOrIndex
arguments.description: For polygons: type(edge), type(vertex), or type(angle). For text/transforms: numeric index. For tables: row number.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: For polygons: 1-based index after type(). For tables: column number (2nd arg).

alternates.description: Extract first edge as line data
Extract second vertex as point data
Extract angle at first vertex (vertex + two adjacent points for angle())
Extract first text item from a select/selectexcept collection
Extract cell from table at (row, col)
Angle data from line for use with angle()

examples.scenario: Extract and draw triangle edges as lines
examples.expression:
T = sas(G, 5, 40, 6, point(G, 0, 0))
line_a = line(G, item(T, type(edge), 1), type(segment), c(blue))
line_b = line(G, item(T, type(edge), 2), type(segment), c(red))
line_c = line(G, item(T, type(edge), 3), type(segment), c(green))

examples.scenario: Extract and draw angles at each vertex
examples.expression:
angle_A = angle(G, item(T, type(angle), 1), arcsize(25))
angle_B = angle(G, item(T, type(angle), 2), arcsize(25))
angle_C = angle(G, item(T, type(angle), 3), arcsize(25))

examples.scenario: Extract triangle edge as line for sine law proof
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(-10, 11.4), size(25), range(-1, 8), grid(noaxes), type(geometry))
triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, 40, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0))
line_c = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 1), type(segment), c(blue), defer())

examples.scenario: Label triangle edge in Pythagorean theorem proof
